TaskNode.Caption: .Introduction
TaskNode.Note:
Introduction
This file contains demo data to illustrate MyLifeOrganized features. For more information refer to the notes of the tasks in this demo.
MyLife Organized (MLO) is a personal task outliner and to-do list manager which will help you to organize your goals, projects and tasks into a tree and generate the to-do list of actions that require immediate attention. This to-do list will be sorted in order of priority to keep you focused on the most important tasks.
To start using MLO please refer to "Getting Started" topic of the help documentation. Press F1 on any control to receive context help.
There are more templates available for MyLife Organized application. Just click menu File->New... and select a template you like. For example there are templates to implement Getting Things Done (GTD http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Getting_Things_Done) methodology.

TaskNode.Caption: Discuss the requirements with the client
Project "X2"
TaskNode.Note:
Projects
You can indicate that a certain task is a Project. All projects are included in a special Projects view. In this view projects are grouped by statuses and are easy to review.
To indicate that a task is a project select "This is a project" on the Project section of task Properties Pane.
Each project has dynamically calculated Completion Percentage. This percentage is based on how many subtasks of the project are completed and what their Task Effort properties are. If a subtask has higher value of Task Effort property it adds more value to the completion percentage for the entire project.
Time required for the entire project read-only property is a sum of Time required for the task property for all subtasks of the project.

TaskNode.Caption: .Project Information
TaskNode.Note:
You can use links to your files and folders: file:\\c:\projects\X2\
You might need to temporarily exclude branch (a task and all of its subtasks) from the ToDo list.
To hide branch in the ToDo list: 1. On the Outline tab, select a task. 2. On the General section of task Properties Pane, select the "Hide the branch in ToDo" check box.
Note: This task and all of its subtasks will appear dimmed on the Outline tab.

TaskNode.Caption: Send Project Status Report
TaskNode.Note:
Complete subtasks in order
You can instruct MLO to place the next subtask of a project in the To-Do List only after previous subtask is completed. This way you can create projects where certain tasks are dependent on the completion of other tasks.
To set complete subtasks in order option for a task: 1. On the Outline tab, select a task. 2. On the General section of task Properties Pane, select the Complete subtasks in order check box.

TaskNode.Caption: Approve the report with my boss
Setup conference call
TaskNode.Note:
Time Required for a Task
You can enter how much time required to accomplish a task. The minimum and maximum values can be entered. This time will be used in the "Time" filter on the To-Do list.
To enter the time required for a task 1. Open Properties Pane by selecting menu View -> Properties Pane 2. Select Properties tab and open Timing & Effort section 3. Enter the minimum and maximum time in the Time required for the task controls.

TaskNode.Caption: Review: Keep an eye of the future
TaskNode.Note:
Goal for week, month and year
MLO helps you to organize your regular goal planning. You can indicate that a certain task is a weekly goal, monthly goal or yearly goal. The special Goals view is designed to easily review all your goals.
The weekly goal is what you should focus your activities on for the week. Monthly goals should be reviewed on a weekly basis to determine specific actions that can be done this week to achieve particular monthly goals. Yearly goals are usually reviewed on a monthly basis to determine if some of them can become goals for the current month.
To change the View: 1. On the Outline tab, click Advanced Views to open the View list. 2. From the View list, select the option you need.

TaskNode.Caption: Find house we like
TaskNode.Note:
Task Effort
You can select how much Effort will take you to complete the task.
This value affects dynamically calculated completion percentage for a Project. This percentage is based on how many subtasks of the project are completed and what their Task Effort properties are. If a task has higher value of Task Effort property it adds more value to completion percentage for the entire project.
Note: This parameter is different from Time required for a task parameter. For example if you just read a book, it would require less effort than if you write MLO help documentation :-). It might take you the same amount of the time to accomplish these tasks but the Effort would be different.

TaskNode.Caption: Take a loan for the house
I am a positive loving person
Call friends about the party
TaskNode.Note:
Context Hours
A context may have hours schedule during which it is open or closed. If a context you select for to-do filter is closed at the moment, the tasks associated with this context will NOT be shown in the To-Do list. Unless you check Include closed check box on To-Do tab.
To set Open/Closed schedule for a context 1. Click Tools->Manage Contexts... and select a context 3. Select Hours tab on the right 4. Select context open/closed schedule in the calendar using mouse pointer.
Note: The green line in the calendar shows the current moment.

TaskNode.Caption: Send link with our vacation photos
Take vitamins
TaskNode.Note:
Reoccurrence
You can set a task to occur repeatedly. For example a task that occurs on a regular basis, such as weekly status reports or periodical car maintenance, can be designed as recurring.

TaskNode.Caption: Sign up for the swimming pool
Drag&Drop items from Outlook to MLO outline
Pares input like "Call Jim tomorrow at 3p"
Study MLO application
TaskNode.Note:
www.myLifeOrganized.net
The links are supported in notes section.

TaskNode.Caption: Buy compact disc
TaskNode.Note:
Understanding of contexts
Many tasks can be completed only in certain places or situations. For example you can complete task "Buy the compact disk" only when you are in the music store. So you can create new contexts "Music store" and assign this place for this task. Now on the To-Do tab in the Contexts filter you can select context "Music store" and you will see only tasks which were assigned to this context. You can also think of the context as of the categories for the tasks.
Each context can also include other contexts in the hierarchy.
Each context can have open/closed schedule.
